blocks.content: For large projects, a single agent with 50 tools will make errors. The Supervisor-Worker pattern breaks this down. A supervisor agent handles triage, extracting intent and delegating sub-tasks to specialized sub-agents with 3-5 tools each.

blocks.title: Architecture
blocks.content: The supervisor agent is the primary endpoint, and it routes payloads to sub-agents via the A2A network protocol. It consolidates the final responses.

Question 1: What is the main benefit of the Supervisor-Worker pattern?
- A) It removes the need for LLMs.
- B) It prevents tool selection confusion by isolating tools within small, focused sub-agents.
- C) It is faster to execute.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Focus limits selection mistakes.
